checks.description: When a legal operation request (opn_valid) is presented and the consumer is ready (res_ready), the module must eventually assert res_valid and present a stable 16‑bit result that does not change while res_valid remains asserted and res_ready stays low.
checks.pass_condition: After opn_valid rises with res_ready high, a later cycle where res_valid is high must be observed; from that point until res_ready goes high, result must remain constant.
checks.trigger_condition: On each rising edge of opn_valid when res_ready is high.

checks.description: For boundary input values (dividend or divisor equal to 0 or 255), the 16‑bit result must respect the defined format: lower 8 bits hold the quotient, upper 8 bits hold the remainder, each fitting within 8 bits.
checks.pass_condition: result[7:0] (quotient) and result[15:8] (remainder) are each 8‑bit values; no overflow beyond 8 bits is observable.
checks.trigger_condition: When opn_valid is asserted with dividend or divisor at their min or max values.
